document_summary: MediaResearch.cn report on the 11th China Communication Conference held at Peking University, July 9-10, 2010. Documents the conference's international scope (260+ scholars from China and abroad), competitive paper selection process (23 papers selected from 200+ submissions), and Liu's participation as a conference speaker. Provides supporting context for Liu's Excellent Paper Award.

snippets.text: From July 9th to 10th, 2010, the 11th China Communication Conference, co- organized by the Communication Association of China, the School of Journalism and Communication of Peking University, and the Institute of Journalism and Communication of the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences, was grandly held at Peking University. More than 260 communication scholars from China and abroad gathered in Yan Garden to discuss the impact and challenges of globalization and networking on society and culture under the theme of "New Media, Multiculturalism, Global Communication: Challenges and Adaptations." Peking University President and Academician of the Chinese Academy of Sciences, Professor Zhou, Qifeng, Executive Vice President of the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences, Researcher Wang, Weiguang, Honorary Chairman of the All- China Journalists Association and Dean of the School of Journalism and Communication of Peking University, Shao, Huaze, and others attended the opening ceremony.
snippets.subject: 11th China Communication Conference
snippets.reasoning: Establishes the prestige and international scope of the conference: co-organized by three major institutions, 260+ scholars from China and abroad, attended by Peking University president and CASS executive vice president.

snippets.text: By the Weiming Lake, summer lotus brings coolness; a gathering of talents, discussing communication extensively. The scale and the number of participants in this conference have exceeded previous ones, with more than 260 Chinese and English papers submitted. Scholars and communication research students from countries such as the United States, the United Kingdom, France, Italy, Canada, Switzerland, Sweden, Japan, South Korea, India, the United Arab Emirates, as well as Hong Kong and Taiwan regions have attended. At the invitation of the conference, renowned American new media expert Professor S. Shyam Sundar, famous cross- cultural communication scholar Professor Milton J. Bennett, and Professor Lucia Montefusco, a rhetoric professor from the Department of Classics at the University of Bologna and current president of the International Society for the History of Rhetoric, attended the conference and delivered keynote speeches.
snippets.subject: 11th China Communication Conference
snippets.reasoning: Quantifies international scope: 260+ papers submitted, scholars from 11+ countries. Names prominent international keynote speakers (Sundar, Bennett, Montefusco), demonstrating the conference's global academic standing.

snippets.text: This two- day conference on communication studies, in an open and inclusive intellectual space, featured rich and thought- provoking viewpoints, novel and diverse perspectives, adding a warm and strong academic atmosphere to the Peking University Yan Garden during the summer. Chinese scholars such as Dai, Yuanguang, Liu, Dehuan, Sun, Yingchun, and Xie, Xinzhou also delivered conference speeches on topics such as international communication, cross- cultural communication, new media research, and communication research methods. Communication scholars and research students from home and abroad participated in the conference's keynote speeches and parallel sessions, discussing topics such as "New Media and Social Development," "Cross- Cultural Communication Theory and Application," "International Communication in Contemporary Chinese Context," "Communication Ethics and Media's Social Responsibility," "New Perspectives and Applications of Communication Research Methods," "Traditional Media Innovation and Social Development," "Rhetoric Communication Research," "Science
snippets.subject: Dehuan Liu
snippets.reasoning: Directly names Liu, Dehuan as one of the Chinese scholars who delivered conference speeches, establishing his active scholarly participation alongside other prominent researchers at the prestigious conference.

snippets.text: The conference organizing committee also organized expert scholars to anonymously evaluate and select 23 excellent academic papers from the more than 200 papers submitted by scholars and students from home and abroad. The award ceremony was held at the closing ceremony.
snippets.subject: 11th China Communication Conference
snippets.reasoning: Key quantitative evidence: 23 papers selected from 200+ submissions through anonymous expert evaluation. Demonstrates the competitive and rigorous selection process for the Excellent Paper Award that Liu received, with an acceptance rate of approximately 11.5%.

snippets.text: The MediaResearch.cn is a professional academic website hosted by the Institute of Journalism and Communication of the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences. The purpose of this website is to exchange communication information, observe communication media, analyze communication phenomena, and discuss communication academics.
snippets.subject: MediaResearch.cn
snippets.reasoning: Establishes the credibility of the reporting source: MediaResearch.cn is hosted by the Institute of Journalism and Communication of the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences, a premier national research institution.
